title: Enterprise Password Policy
content:
Enterprise Password Policy v3.2 — Aligned with NIST SP 800-63B and DoD STIG requirements.

1. PASSWORD LENGTH AND COMPLEXITY
1.1 All user passwords must be a minimum of 14 characters in length.
1.2 Passwords must contain at least three of the following four character classes: uppercase letters (A-Z), lowercase letters (a-z), digits (0-9), and special characters (!@#$%^&*()_+-=).
1.3 Service accounts and administrative accounts must use passwords of at least 20 characters.
1.4 Passphrases are encouraged. A passphrase of 20+ characters with mixed case may satisfy complexity requirements without special characters, per NIST SP 800-63B guidance.

2. PASSWORD EXPIRATION AND ROTATION
2.1 Standard user passwords must be rotated every 90 days.
2.2 Privileged and administrative account passwords must be rotated every 60 days.
2.3 Service account passwords must be rotated every 180 days or upon personnel change, whichever comes first.
2.4 Per NIST SP 800-63B, organizations may adopt longer expiration periods (up to 365 days) if continuous monitoring and breach detection are in place.
2.5 Passwords must be changed immediately upon suspicion of compromise.

3. PASSWORD HISTORY AND REUSE
3.1 The system must enforce a password history of at least 24 previous passwords.
3.2 Users may not reuse any of their previous 24 passwords.
3.3 A minimum password age of 1 day must be enforced to prevent rapid cycling through the password history.

4. ACCOUNT LOCKOUT
4.1 Accounts must be locked after 5 consecutive failed login attempts.
4.2 Account lockout duration must be a minimum of 30 minutes or until an administrator manually unlocks the account.
4.3 The failed login attempt counter must reset after 30 minutes of no failed attempts.
4.4 Administrative accounts must be locked after 3 consecutive failed attempts and require manual unlock by a security administrator.

5. PASSWORD STORAGE AND TRANSMISSION
5.1 Passwords must be stored using approved one-way cryptographic hashing algorithms: bcrypt (cost factor 12+), scrypt, or Argon2id.
5.2 Plaintext password storage is strictly prohibited.
5.3 Passwords must only be transmitted over encrypted channels (TLS 1.2 or higher).
5.4 Password hashes must be salted with a unique, randomly generated salt of at least 128 bits per NIST guidelines.

6. PROHIBITED PASSWORDS
6.1 Passwords must be checked against a list of commonly used, expected, or compromised passwords. This list must include passwords from prior breach corpuses (e.g., Have I Been Pwned).
6.2 Dictionary words, usernames, and variations of the company name are prohibited.
6.3 Sequential patterns (12345, abcdef) and repeated characters (aaaa, 1111) are prohibited.

7. MULTI-FACTOR AUTHENTICATION INTEGRATION
7.1 Passwords alone are not sufficient for access to sensitive systems. MFA must be enabled per the Multi-Factor Authentication Policy (SEC-002).
7.2 Password requirements may be relaxed for accounts using phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn) per NIST SP 800-63B Level 3.

8. REFERENCES
- NIST SP 800-63B Digital Identity Guidelines
- DoD STIG V-254391 Password Complexity
- CIS Controls v8 Control 5.2
- ISO 27001:2022 Annex A.9.4.3
